comment.name: Comment
comment.text:
Morning *Update, 8:00 AM

Good morning, Sugarloafers.

That blizzard that body-slammed southern New England yesterday swung by on its way out and dropped off 2-3 inches of fresh snow.

The wind generously gathered all the snow into drifts that'll be super fun to find. Hint: check skier's left.

Winds are hanging around this morning but will gradually ease up through the day. The top of Skyline is reporting 4°F with northwest winds at 20 to 25 mph, gusting into the 30s. Skyline and SuperQuad look good to spin, but King Pine (*and Timberline) are on wind hold. Lift Ops is evaluating all other lifts. Keep an eye on lift status for live updates. Will is ON IT.

Temperatures are expected to top out around 14°F up high and 19°F at the base. It's mostly cloudy, and the summit is a bit socked in at the moment, but the sun is definitely trying to break through, so check the webcams before heading up.

Groomers worked their way through the drifts overnight, smoothing things out on Widowmaker, Flume, King's Landing, Spillway, Sluice from the top, and more. Scroll for the full lineup and plan your corduroy tour accordingly.'

Take advantage of $75.00 1-hour private lessons starting today and every Tuesday through the rest of the season. Visit the Snowsports Desk in the base lodge or hop online to book.

Be sure to head over to The Birches this afternoon from 3-6 PM for FREE TUBING! Just make sure you visit Guest Services to sign a waiver, and you're good to go.

Taco Tuesday happy hour runs from 2 to 5 PM at the Widowmaker with $10 margaritas, $5 Coronas, and $5 tacos. Arrrrriba!

Enjoy the ride,
Alex

comment.name: Uphill
comment.text:
Uphill travel is open to Spillway X-Cut via the West Mountain Route, the SuperQuad Route, and the Bucksaw Route.

Visit the Uphill Policy page for detailed directions.
 
Note: All day-ticket uphill travelers must pick up their 1-day wristband prior to starting their ascent with Guest Services located in the base lodge. Your wristband must be visible at all times. For more information, visit the Uphill Policy page.

trail.name: Lower Sheer Boom
trail.comment: Please note Lower Sheer Boom refers to the section between Tote X-Cut and Peavy X-Cut

trail.name: Upper Stub's Trail
trail.comment: MTB trails need to fill in a little more

park.comment: "Fun Zone" featuring 16 low-angle bumps, spines, rollers, and banked turns
